description: forward a governed claim to a node — submit a sub-claim to the node's govd, run it under the node's per-step oversight, return the verdict + ledger pointer
rules: the agent only KNOWs + NAMES the claim; the node's govd governs (bless / push_back / refuse) and grants every step
execution is faithful under the node's non-root identity (the executor's no-root gate) — never root
a destructive sub-claim push_backs until APPROVE waives the named rule; the verdict + ledger pointer are recorded, never the task data
usage: Looks for the govd (NODE_URL, else $GOVD_URL, else the local node) and probes its /health for exec_mode, then drives the matching flow: a delegated body executes each step via its own exod (server-side, signed); a cooperative anchor runs them caller-side. Set SUB_LEDGER (abs path to the sub-claim task-ledger) + optional APPROVE (space-separated rule ids). Output: run.json — node, target skill/perk, exec_mode, decision, plan_sha, ledger url, ok.
limitation: Needs a reachable node + a registry matching the node's blessed chip. Drives infra.govern.govd_client (run_governed | run_delegated by the node's exec_mode) — not portable like discover. For a delegated body the node's exod is the executor (the agent runs nothing).
